Critical bugs discovered during hands-on testing of Phase 2.

### Todos

#### 15. CodeLens Summary — Click-to-Show Rich HTML Panel

> *Moved from Phase 2 (#12) — rolled back during Phase 3 debugging*

**Problem:** Currently the CodeLens summary item has:
- **Hover:** Shows plain-text tooltip via `Command.tooltip` (string-only, no formatting)
- **Click:** No-op (`showCommentTooltip` command does nothing)

The user wants the opposite: **no tooltip on hover**, **rich HTML content shown on click**.

**Root cause:** Phase 1's spike confirmed `Command.tooltip` only accepts strings. This led to a plain-text tooltip as the "best effort" — but the intended UX is click-triggered rich content, not hover.

**Approach:** Use `vscode.window.createWebviewPanel()` to show a themed HTML panel when clicking the CodeLens summary. The extension already has webview infrastructure (`anchorsGridProvider.ts`, `anchorsGridWebview.ts`) so this pattern is established.

**Changes:**
1. **Remove tooltip from CodeLens summary** — delete the `tooltip` property from the summary CodeLens item in `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`
2. **Implement `showCommentTooltip` command** — replace the no-op with logic that:
   - Receives `(uri, startLine)` arguments
   - Finds the comment block at that location
   - Renders it as styled HTML (theme-aware colors for method names, params, types, etc.)
   - Opens a `WebviewPanel` (or reuses an existing one) with the rendered content
3. **Create `renderToHtml()` function** — new function in `commentRenderer.ts` that produces styled HTML with:
   - Theme-aware colors (method name, parameter, type, comment text colors)
   - Proper section layout (Summary, Parameters table, Returns, Remarks, Example with code styling, Exceptions)
   - CSS that respects VS Code theme variables (`--vscode-editor-foreground`, etc.)
4. **Panel behavior:**
   - Reuse a single panel (don't open a new one per click)
   - Panel title: method/member name or "XML Documentation"
   - Panel closes on Escape or when clicking a different CodeLens updates the content in-place

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`, `src/extension.ts`

#### 16. Native CodeLens Tooltip — Investigate Suppression

**Problem:** When hovering over the CodeLens summary text, VS Code shows a native tooltip displaying the command title. This is VS Code's built-in behavior for all CodeLens items — it always shows the `Command.title` string as a tooltip.

**Root cause:** VS Code's CodeLens rendering always shows a hover tooltip. There is no official API to suppress it. Setting `Command.tooltip` to `''` (empty string) may suppress the tooltip text, though VS Code may still show the command title as fallback.

**Investigation:**
- Test setting `tooltip: ''` on the summary CodeLens `Command` — this is the most likely way to suppress
- If that doesn't work, test `tooltip: '\u200B'` (zero-width space) as a workaround
- Document findings either way

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`

---

#### 17. Flickering / Crash When Typing in Comments

**Problem:** Typing inside an XML doc comment (e.g., starting `NOTE:`) causes the entire comment block to flicker rapidly. Status bar icons ("analyzing files", "test discover") also flicker. The extension eventually crashes and rendering mode switches to OFF.

**Root cause:** The `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler in `extension.ts` (line 256) calls THREE decoration systems on EVERY keystroke with NO debouncing:

```
decorationManager?.onDocumentChanged(editor, event);  // ✅ Has internal 1500ms suppression
anchorDecorationManager?.updateDecorations(editor);    // ❌ NO debounce — full document rescan
prefixHighlighter?.updateDecorations(editor);          // ❌ NO debounce — full document rescan
```

`anchorDecorationManager.updateDecorations()` scans every line of the document, checks for anchor tags, rebuilds all decoration ranges, and re-applies them. On every single keystroke. Same for `prefixHighlighter`. This causes a cascade of rapid decoration disposal + re-creation that causes visible flickering and can overwhelm VS Code's rendering pipeline.

Additionally, the `debouncedAnchorRescan` (line 264) triggers workspace-level anchor scanning on each edit, adding more load.

**Fix:** Add debouncing to the `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler for `anchorDecorationManager` and `prefixHighlighter`:
1. Create debounce timers for anchor decorations and prefix highlighting (same pattern as `decorationManager.editTimers`)
2. On text change: clear existing timers, set new timers (e.g., 500ms delay)
3. Only call `updateDecorations()` when the timer fires
4. This matches how `decorationManager` already handles it (1500ms `EDIT_SUPPRESSION_DELAY`)

**Files:** `src/extension.ts`

---

#### 18. `</para><para>` Text Concatenation Bug — "NIf"

**Problem:** When XML content contains `</para><para>` (adjacent para elements), text from consecutive paragraphs is concatenated without spacing in the CodeLens summary. Example: paragraph ending "...N" followed by paragraph starting "If..." renders as "NIf" in the summary.

**Root cause:** `extractPlainText()` in `commentRenderer.ts` (line 1103) joins all child text parts with `parts.join('')` — empty string separator. When processing a `<summary>` containing multiple `<para>` elements, the text from each paragraph is concatenated directly:
- Para 1 text: `"...something N"`
- Para 2 text: `"If condition..."`  
- Result: `"...something NIf condition..."`

The full-content rendering (`renderCommentBlock`) handles this correctly by inserting blank lines around `<para>` tags (lines 596-599), but the summary extraction (`getStrippedSummaryFromXml` → `extractPlainText`) bypasses that rendering path entirely and does raw text extraction.

**Fix:** Change `extractPlainText()` to add a space when recursing into block-level elements like `<para>`:
- For `<para>` children: push `' '` before the child's text
- This ensures word boundaries are preserved in the plain-text summary
- Alternative: change `parts.join('')` to `parts.join(' ')` but this would over-space inline elements like `<c>`, `<see>`, `<paramref>`
- Better approach: handle `<para>` specifically in the switch statement, prepending a space

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 19. Documentation Overlay on Code Anchors Pane

**Problem:** Clicking the CodeLens summary opens a full `WebviewPanel` in a new editor column. The user wants a lightweight popup that appears within the existing Code Anchors pane and dismisses on click-away.

**Approach:** Overlay on the existing Code Anchors `WebviewView`:
1. Click CodeLens summary → extension focuses Code Anchors pane via command, then sends `postMessage` with rendered doc HTML to the anchors grid webview
2. The webview JavaScript shows a floating overlay card on top of the grid, sized dynamically to pane height with scrollbar if content exceeds available space
3. Dismiss triggers: clicking outside overlay, clicking ✕ button, pressing Escape
4. On dismiss: overlay hides, focus returns to code editor via `postMessage` → extension calls `showTextDocument`
5. Clicking a different CodeLens summary updates overlay content in-place

**Changes:**
- **`src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`**: Add overlay container HTML, CSS (position: absolute, z-index, dynamic height, overflow-y: auto, backdrop click handler), and JS (show/hide/escape/dismiss logic, postMessage for focus-return)
- **`src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts`**: Add `showDocOverlay(html: string)` method that posts a `showDocOverlay` message to the webview. Add message handler for `dismissOverlay` that returns focus to editor.
- **`src/extension.ts`**: Replace `WebviewPanel` logic in `showCommentTooltip` command with: focus Code Anchors pane, call `anchorsGridProvider.showDocOverlay(html)`. Remove `docPanel` variable entirely.
- **`src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`**: `renderToHtml()` should return just the body content (no `<!DOCTYPE>`, `<html>`, `<head>`) since it'll be injected into the existing webview's overlay div. CSS styles can be embedded inline or in a `<style>` block within the content.

**Files:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`, `src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts`, `src/extension.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 20. Documentation Overlay — Formatting Per User Spec

**Problem:** The rendered HTML doesn't match the user's desired formatting. Based on screenshots and the original spec from Phase 1 (#8), the desired format is:

```
MethodName: Summary text here

    param1 — Description of first parameter
    param2 — Description of second parameter

Returns: Description of return value

Remarks:
    Remarks content here

Example:
    Example code here
```

**Key formatting requirements:**
- Method name prominently displayed with summary inline
- Parameters indented with `—` separator
- Section labels (Returns, Remarks, Example) as simple headers, not uppercase/bordered
- Compact and readable — no heavy visual chrome
- Theme-aware colors for different semantic elements (method name, params, types)

**Fix:** Rewrite `renderToHtml()` CSS and layout to match the spec:
- Simplify section labels — plain bold text, no uppercase/borders/letter-spacing
- Keep parameter layout (already close to spec with `name — description`)
- Simplify example styling (indented code, not heavy blockquote)
- Remove excessive margins and visual weight
- Content will render inside the overlay div on the anchors grid pane

**Files:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

#### 21. Anchor Tag Colorization in Rendered Comments

**Problem:** Anchor tags (TODO, HACK, NOTE, BUG, etc.) appearing within XML doc comment text are not colorized in either the CodeLens summary or the HTML documentation panel. The live editor colorizes them via `AnchorDecorationManager`, but the rendered output treats them as plain text.

**Root cause:** `processMarkdownInText()` in `markdownProcessor.ts` handles markdown formatting (bold, italic, code, links) but does not detect anchor tag patterns. The `segmentToHtml()` function has no anchor-aware segment type. The `AnchorDecorationManager` only works on live editor decorations, not on rendered output.

**Fix:** Add anchor tag detection to the rendering pipeline:
1. Add `SegmentType.Anchor` to the `SegmentType` enum in `types.ts`
2. Add `anchorColor?: string` to `RenderedSegment` interface
3. In `processMarkdownInText()` (or a post-processing step), detect anchor patterns (TODO:, HACK:, NOTE:, BUG:, FIXME:, UNDONE:, REVIEW:, ANCHOR:) in text segments and split them into `Anchor` segments with their configured color
4. In `segmentToHtml()`, render `Anchor` segments with inline color styling using the anchor's configured color from `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES`
5. In `segmentToMarkdown()`, render with bold/color if applicable

**Files:** `src/types.ts`, `src/rendering/markdownProcessor.ts`, `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

2. **#18** Para concatenation — parser bug, targeted fix
3. **#16** Tooltip suppression — quick investigation
4. **#15** Click-to-show rich HTML panel — implement `showCommentTooltip` command
5. **#19** Doc overlay on anchors pane — replace WebviewPanel with overlay UX (depends on #15)
6. **#20** Overlay formatting — CSS/layout rework (depends on #19)
7. **#21** Anchor colorization — new feature, spans multiple files (can start parallel with #19)
8. Update README.md after all changes

---

### Phase 3: Summary of Changes ✓

#### #16 — Tooltip Suppression
Set `tooltip: ''` (empty string) on the summary CodeLens `Command` object in `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`. This suppresses the VS Code native hover tooltip that was showing the command title text. VS Code still renders the CodeLens text itself, but the hover popup is eliminated.

#### #17 — Flickering Fix (Critical)
**Root cause:** In `extension.ts`, the `onDidChangeTextDocument` handler was calling `anchorDecorationManager.updateDecorations()` and `prefixHighlighter.updateDecorations()` directly on every keystroke with NO debounce. Each call performed a full document rescan, causing visible flickering and performance degradation that could crash the extension to OFF state.

**Fix:** Added 500ms debounce timers (`anchorDecUpdateTimer` and `prefixDecUpdateTimer`) in `extension.ts`. Both decoration update calls are now wrapped in `setTimeout` with `DECORATION_UPDATE_DELAY = 500` ms, using the same re-capture pattern for the active editor. The `decorationManager.onDocumentChanged()` call remains direct since it has its own internal 1500ms edit suppression.

#### #18 — Para Concatenation Fix
**Root cause:** `extractPlainText()` in `commentRenderer.ts` had no handling for `<para>` elements — they fell through to the `default` case which recursively extracted text but with no separator. When `<summary>` contained `</para><para>` patterns, text from adjacent paragraphs concatenated directly (e.g., "N" + "If" = "NIf").

**Fix:** Added explicit `case 'para':` in the `extractPlainText()` switch statement that prepends a space (`' '`) before recursively extracting the child text. This ensures word boundaries between paragraph elements.

#### #19 — Documentation Overlay on Anchors Pane
**Replaced:** The `WebviewPanel` approach (which opened a new editor tab) with a floating overlay card on the existing Code Anchors grid webview.

**Changes:**
- Added overlay HTML/CSS/JS to `anchorsGridWebview.ts`:
  - Semi-transparent backdrop (`rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`) that dismisses on click
  - Centered overlay card with max 600px width, dynamic height with scrollbar
  - Close button (✕) in top-right corner
  - Keyboard dismiss on Escape
  - All VS Code theme variables for consistent theming
- Added `showDocOverlay(html)` method to `AnchorsGridProvider` class
- Added `overlayDismissed` message handler that returns focus to active editor
- Rewired `showCommentTooltip` command in `extension.ts`:
  - Focuses the anchors grid pane via `kat-comment-studio.anchorsGrid.focus`
  - Sends HTML fragment via `showDocOverlay()` instead of creating a WebviewPanel
  - Uses new `renderToHtmlFragment()` (body content only, no HTML wrapper)
- Removed `docPanel` module variable and `renderToHtml` import from `extension.ts`

#### #20 — Overlay Formatting
Added new `renderToHtmlFragment()` export to `commentRenderer.ts` that returns just the HTML body content without the `<!DOCTYPE>` wrapper. This is used by the overlay which has its own CSS defined in the anchors grid webview. The full-document `renderToHtml()` was refactored to call `renderToHtmlFragment()` internally, eliminating code duplication. Overlay CSS in `anchorsGridWebview.ts` includes themed styles for all section types (summary, params, returns, remarks, example, exceptions, see-also).

#### #21 — Anchor Tag Colorization in Rendered HTML
**Added:** Automatic colorization of anchor tags (TODO:, HACK:, NOTE:, BUG:, FIXME:, UNDONE:, REVIEW:, ANCHOR:) in rendered HTML output.

**Implementation:**
- Imported `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES` into `commentRenderer.ts`
- Built a static color lookup (`ANCHOR_TAG_COLORS`) from the anchor service definitions
- Added `colorizeAnchorTags()` helper function that wraps matched tags with `<span style="color:...;font-weight:600">`
- Applied in `segmentToHtml()` for `SegmentType.Text` segments — all text content now has anchor tags colorized using the same colors as inline editor decorations

### Files Modified (Phase 3)

- `src/extension.ts` — Added debounce timers for anchor/prefix decoration updates; rewired showCommentTooltip to use overlay; removed docPanel and renderToHtml import
- `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts` — Added `tooltip: ''` to suppress native CodeLens hover
- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` — Fixed `<para>` handling in `extractPlainText()`; added `renderToHtmlFragment()` export; added anchor tag colorization in `segmentToHtml()`; imported `BUILTIN_ANCHOR_TYPES`
- `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts` — Added overlay HTML structure, CSS styles, and JS dismiss logic (backdrop click, close button, Escape key)
- `src/anchors/anchorsGridProvider.ts` — Added `showDocOverlay()` method and `overlayDismissed` message handler

### Test Results

- All 157 tests passing
- TypeScript compilation clean
- No new dependencies added
